title: psychotria costivenia subsp. clementis
intro: Psychotria costivenia subsp. clementis, also known as Clements' wild coffee, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psychotria costivenia subsp. clementis include its potential to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. The herbal preparation of the plant is often made into a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
